title: Supplier API Migration Guide: Upgrading from v2 to v3
content:
## Supplier API v2 to v3 Migration Guide

### Background
The supplier officially decommissioned the v2 API endpoint on 2026-03-20; all requests will return 404. The new v3 API is live and offers enhanced functionality.

### Migration Steps
1. In the configuration management system, change INT-1502 (Supplier API v3) status from inactive to active
2. Change INT-1501 (Supplier API v2) status to inactive or deprecated
3. Update JOB-1501 (Supplier Data Sync) data source configuration to point to the v3 endpoint
4. Run a manual sync test to confirm v3 connectivity is working
5. Monitor error rate over the next 24 hours to ensure it stays below 0.01

### v3 Change Notes
- Added batch query endpoint supporting up to 500 records per request
- Authentication method unchanged (API Key), but a new key is required (already configured in INT-1502)
- Response format changed from XML to JSON
- Pagination mechanism changed to cursor-based

### Important Notes
- Back up the current configuration before migrating
- The v2 endpoint is no longer available; rollback is not possible
- For issues, contact supplier technical support: supplier-support@example.com

title: Automation Task Dependency Documentation
content:
## Automation Task Dependency Chain Documentation

### Supply Chain Automation Task Chain
The following three tasks have strict dependency relationships:

```
JOB-1501 (Supplier Data Sync) -> JOB-1502 (Inventory Auto Update) -> JOB-1503 (Auto Restock Trigger)
```

- **JOB-1501 Supplier Data Sync**: Pulls the latest product pricing, inventory levels, and delivery schedules from the supplier API. Runs every 4 hours.
- **JOB-1502 Inventory Auto Update**: Updates quantities and statuses in the local inventory system based on supplier data pulled by JOB-1501. Depends on JOB-1501's data; if JOB-1501 fails, the data source is unavailable.
- **JOB-1503 Auto Restock Trigger**: Checks safety stock levels based on the latest inventory data and automatically generates restock orders for items below threshold. Depends on JOB-1502's update results; refuses to execute if inventory data has not been updated for more than 24 hours (to prevent ordering based on stale data).

### Failure Propagation
When JOB-1501 fails, it triggers a cascade:
1. JOB-1502 fails because it cannot retrieve upstream data
2. JOB-1503 fails because inventory data is stale
3. Affected inventory products cannot be automatically restocked
4. Warehouse and Procurement departments may report data inconsistency issues

### Repair Priority
Repairs should start from the root node of the dependency chain (JOB-1501). Once the data source is restored, downstream tasks will automatically resume normal operation.

title: Inventory Data Desync Repair Procedure
content:
## Inventory Data Desync Repair Procedure

### Applicable Scenarios
- Recovery after prolonged auto-sync task failure
- Inventory system data does not match actual stock
- Urgent inventory data update required

### Repair Steps
1. **Confirm sync tasks have been restored**: Verify that JOB-1501 and JOB-1502 are running normally again
2. **Manually trigger a full sync**:
   - Log in to the admin console
   - Navigate to Data Sync > Manual Trigger
   - Select Full Sync (not incremental)
   - Estimated duration: 10-15 minutes
3. **Reconcile key products**:
   - Compare system inventory against physical count data
   - Focus on: products showing 0 stock, products below safety level
4. **Check auto-restock queue**:
   - After sync completes, check whether new restock orders have been auto-generated
   - Manually trigger JOB-1503 to check restocking needs
5. **Notify relevant departments**:
   - Warehouse: confirm inventory data has been updated
   - Procurement: confirm restock orders have been generated

### Preventive Measures
- Set up sync failure alerts; if no sync occurs for over 12 hours, automatically notify ops
- Periodically check API integration configuration status
